raw_consumer_statement:
I am writing to formally respond to Amex 's denial of my dispute under the XXXXday billing error rule. While I understand Regulation XXXX  XXXX ( b ) requires consumers to report billing errors within XXXX days, I believe this requirement is not applicable in this case due to the nature of the transaction. 
The charge in question relates to a future delivery of goods ( through a XXXX campaign ) which had an estimated delivery date several months after the charge date. This date continues to be pushed out. Under Regulation XXXX  XXXX ( c ), cardholders retain the right to assert claims and defenses against the issuer when a merchant fails to deliver goods or services, particularly when the purchase was made by internet or mail, which waives the geographic restriction. ( This claim is similar to if I had purchased a flight XXXX months in the future but the airline canceled the flight without refunding me. I would not have known within XXXX days of the charge hitting my statement that I would not receive the service [ flight ] from the airline that is still XXXX months in the future. ) I attempted to resolve this dispute with the campaign organizer directly ( both via email and via message on XXXXXXXX XXXX website ) and only initiated this claim when it became clear that the product would not be delivered as promised. Since the product has still not been delivered, I am asserting my rights under XXXX ( c ) and request that you reconsider your position accordingly. 
Please note that I am not asserting a billing error under XXXX, but a claims and defenses dispute under XXXX ( c ). Thank you for your attention to this matter.
